{"id":"chatcmpl-f5f975f1-eec7-4944-8eb7-1af2237d0bd1","object":"chat.completion","created":1782197347,"model":"default","choices":[{"index":0,"message":{"content":"Scientific notation is a way of writing very large or very small numbers. A number is written as the product of a number between 1 and 10 and an exponential expression that represents a power of 10. For example, the number 654,000 can be written as 6.54 × 105, and the number 0.00654 can be written as 6.54 × 10−3. Numbers written in scientific notation follow these rules:\r\n- The coefficient is a number between 1 and 10.\r\n- The exponential expression is the power of 10 that makes the overall number equal to the given value.\r\n- If the overall number is negative, the power of 10 is negative. For example, 6.02 × 10−23 is the number that expresses Avogadro’s number.\r\n- If the overall number has more than one digit to the left of the decimal point, the value of the coefficient is greater than 10. For example, 123,000,000 can be written as 1.23 × 108.\r\n- If the overall number has more than one digit to the right of the decimal point, the value of the coefficient is less than 10. For example, 0.00432 can be written as 4.32 × 10−3.\n\nWhat is the scientific notation for 0.0000153?Click to select and submit other inputs.\n\n0.0000153 = 1.53 × 10−5\n\nWhat is the scientific notation for 3,700?Click to select and submit other inputs.\n\n3,700 = 3.7 × 103\n\nWhat is the scientific notation for 2.3 × 103?Click to select and submit other inputs.\n\n2.3 × 103 = 2,300\n\nWhat is the scientific notation for 2.3 × 10−3?Click to select and submit other inputs.\n\n2.3 × 10−3 = 0.0023\n\nWhat is the scientific notation for 0.0000023?Click to select and submit other inputs.\n\n0.0000023 = 2.3 × 10−6\n\nWhat is the scientific notation for 0.","role":"assistant"},"finish_reason":"stop","logprobs":null}],"usage":{"prompt_tokens":1620,"completion_tokens":820,"total_tokens":2440}}
